release-date.comment: First public coverage and availability July 2023.

status.comment: Still listed on the official product page and shipped by major resellers as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Orange Pi publishes only PDF schematics, not PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license, so per RESEARCH.md this does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed and HQ'd by Shenzhen Xunlong Software, Shenzhen, Guangdong, China.

process-node.comment: Allwinner does not publish the H618 process node officially; 28 nm is the value reported by third-party SoC databases and matches the closely related H616.

vulkan-support.comment: Vendor advertises Vulkan 1.1 via Mali-G31 MP2; Linux usability depends on the proprietary blob driver.

hw-video-decode.comment: H.265 up to 6Kp30, VP9 up to 6Kp30, H.264 up to 4Kp30 per the H618 SoC datasheet summary. AV1 not supported.

hw-video-encode.comment: H618 VPU advertises H.264 (up to 4Kp25) and H.265 (up to 1080p60) hardware encoding; only I/P frames in the H.264 path.

ram-max-gb.comment: Sold in 1 / 1.5 / 2 / 4 GB variants. The 1.5 GB SKU has known memory-detection issues under mainline kernels.

emmc.comment: Onboard storage is 16 MB SPI flash only; no eMMC.

ethernet.comment: Gigabit Ethernet via Motorcomm YT8531C PHY.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: All USB ports are USB 2.0 only.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: One USB-A 2.0 port on the board. Two additional USB 2.0 lines are exposed on the 13-pin header but require an adapter board, so they are not counted as on-board ports.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: The USB-C port is power-only (5V/3A); no data role.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack on board; analog audio out is only available via the optional 13-pin expansion adapter.

gpio-pins.comment: Main expansion is a 26-pin header. A separate 13-pin header carries TV-out, USB, audio, and IR; not counted here.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 26 pins (not the standard 40-pin RPi pinout). Some I2C/SPI/UART/GPIO functions overlap loosely with the original RPi Zero 26-pin layout but the mapping is not pin-compatible with modern RPi HATs.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: 26-pin header, no HAT EEPROM, mounting holes do not match the RPi standard.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: 26-pin header exposes I2C, SPI, and multiple UARTs (including a 3-pin debug UART).

power-input.comment: USB Type-C connector, data lines not wired.

idle-power-w.comment: Derived from a 30-minute USB-meter measurement of 820 mWh with Wi-Fi and Ethernet enabled (≈1.64 W average). Measured 2026-02 by neonhero.dev.

load-power-w.comment: Derived from a 30-minute stress test reading 1750 mWh with all four cores at 100% (≈3.5 W average). Measured 2026-02 by neonhero.dev.

cooling-required.comment: At ~3.5 W full-load draw the board is widely run without active cooling; vendor does not require a heatsink, though one is recommended for sustained heavy loads.

form-factor-class.comment: 55×50 mm — neither the 65×30 mm Pi Zero footprint nor the 85×56 mm Pi standard.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting holes are Orange Pi Zero–specific, not the RPi or RPi Zero pattern.

weight-g.comment: Vendor product page does not publish a weight. Comparable Allwinner H618 boards in this footprint are typically 25–30 g but no measured figure was found.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainlined since Linux 6.6; Armbian ships stable images on the 6.12 kernel branch as of 2026-04. The 1.5 GB RAM SKU has a memory-detection quirk that is not yet fixed upstream.

official-os-image.comment: Vendor publishes Android 12 TV, Debian 11/12, and Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 images.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor: Debian and Ubuntu. Armbian: community-supported with multiple desktop, server, and minimal images.

long-term-support.comment: Orange Pi does not publish a formal support window. Mainline upstreaming and active Armbian community support extend practical lifetime, but vendor commitment beyond initial OS images is not stated.

android-support.comment: Official Android 12 TV image provided by the vendor.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch price for the 1 GB SKU. Checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: 4 GB SKU sells in the $25–$30 range at retail (Amazon, AliExpress) as of 2026-04-26; vendor launch listing was around $25.

available-regions.comment: Sold worldwide via the Orange Pi AliExpress store and major resellers (Amazon NA/EU, Newegg).

typical-lead-time.comment: Multiple SKUs in stock at Amazon and Newegg as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Official wiki page exists with user manual and schematic PDFs, but coverage is uneven and largely Chinese-first; community resources fill many gaps.

community-size.comment: Orange Pi sits in the broad Rockchip/Allwinner SBC community served by Armbian and r/orangepi; well below RPi but clearly above niche vendors.

active-forum-discord.comment: Orange Pi runs an official forum (orangepi.org/orangepibbsen) with regular activity, plus heavy presence on the Armbian forum.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vendor sells a matching 13-pin expansion board, a few cases, and a heatsink. No HAT-style ecosystem because the header is non-standard 26-pin.
